description:
Build patient self-service subscription cancellation that matches the Terms promise and keeps Stripe, MDI, and the local mirror aligned.

**Scope**
- Add an authenticated account or dashboard cancellation entry point for active subscriptions with patient-safe copy and confirmation states.
- Define whether launch cancellation is immediate or period-end, and encode the selected behavior in Stripe calls, local mirror updates, and evidence records.
- Trigger any required MDI-side cancellation/pause action exactly once when the care workflow requires it.
- Handle duplicate clicks, already-canceled subscriptions, Stripe failures, stale local mirrors, and missing subscription linkage without creating PHI-heavy support notes.
- Keep cancellation reasons bounded or omit free text for launch; do not place clinical content, questionnaire answers, medication names, or MDI workflow URLs/tokens in Stripe metadata or logs.

**Acceptance**
- A signed-in patient with an active subscription can cancel from the account/dashboard surface without calling support.
- Cancellation updates Stripe and the local billing mirror idempotently, with minimal evidence written once.
- MDI-side cancellation/pause side effects run exactly once when required, and ambiguous failures route to support/ops review.
- Dashboard billing state reflects canceled or pending-cancel status with patient-safe messaging.
- Tests cover duplicate requests, already-canceled state, Stripe failure, MDI side-effect failure, and no-PHI metadata/logging.
title: Patient self-service subscription cancellation
